seoTitle: On-Bright Power Management IC Products | AC-DC DC-DC LED Driver | BeiLuo Electronics
seoDescription: Browse On-Bright power management ICs including AC-DC converters, DC-DC converters, LED drivers, and battery management solutions. Industrial-grade power ICs for various applications.

categories.name: AC-DC Converters
categories.description: Offline power supply controllers and converters for adapter and power supply applications
categories.longDescription: On-Bright AC-DC converters provide high-efficiency offline power solutions for adapters, chargers, and power supplies. Products include quasi-resonant flyback controllers, CCM/DCM flyback controllers, and PFC pre-regulators with integrated high-voltage MOSFETs. These ICs feature low standby power, comprehensive protection, and reduced external component count for cost-effective designs.

series.name: OB22xx Series
series.description: Quasi-resonant flyback controllers for high-efficiency adapters

series.name: OB23xx Series
series.description: CCM/DCM flyback controllers for general purpose power supplies

categories.selectionGuide: Choose OB22xx series for high-efficiency adapter applications requiring low EMI. Select OB23xx series for cost-sensitive designs with good efficiency. Consider power rating, topology, and protection features based on your application requirements.

products.name: OB2263 PWM Controller
products.shortDescription: High-performance PWM controller for offline power supplies with low standby power
descriptionParagraphs: The OB2263 is a highly integrated PWM controller designed for offline power supply applications. It features a high-voltage startup circuit, built-in leading edge blanking, and comprehensive protection functions including OVP, OCP, and OTP.

With its low startup current and operating current, the OB2263 achieves excellent standby power performance, meeting international energy efficiency standards. The internal frequency jittering helps reduce EMI, simplifying filter design and reducing system cost.
The OB2263 is ideal for adapter applications, open-frame power supplies, and auxiliary power supplies where cost-effectiveness and reliability are critical requirements.
features: Built-in frequency jittering for EMI reduction

faeReview.content: The OB2263 is a workhorse PWM controller that I've used in countless adapter designs. Its high integration reduces BOM cost significantly - the built-in high-voltage startup eliminates external startup resistors. The frequency jittering really works for EMI; I've passed CISPR22 Class B with minimal filtering. Standby power is excellent, easily meeting DoE Level VI requirements. One tip: pay attention to the current sense resistor layout - keep the trace to the CS pin short and away from the MOSFET drain to avoid noise issues. Overall, a reliable, cost-effective choice for sub-60W applications.
faeReview.author: Senior FAE - Power Supply Applications

alternativeParts.reason: Industry-standard PWM controller with wider availability
alternativeParts.comparison: UC3842 requires external startup circuit and has higher standby power; OB2263 offers better integration and lower standby power at competitive cost

alternativeParts.reason: Similar feature set with skip-cycle mode for light load efficiency
alternativeParts.comparison: NCP1252 offers skip-cycle mode; OB2263 provides better cost efficiency for standard applications

companionParts.description: Synchronous rectifier controller for improved efficiency
USB Power Delivery controller for adapter applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum output power of OB2263?
faqs.answer: The OB2263 can support up to 60W output power in flyback topology with appropriate external MOSFET and transformer design. For higher power applications, consider using external MOSFETs with higher current ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 20-30% power margin below maximum rating for reliable operation and thermal management.

faqs.question: How do I achieve low standby power with OB2263?
faqs.answer: OB2263 achieves low standby power through its low startup current and operating current design. Use a high-value startup resistor (2M-4M ohm range) and minimize the VCC capacitor value while ensuring stable operation. The built-in frequency reduction at light load also helps reduce switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference design guidelines for startup resistor and VCC capacitor selection.

faqs.question: What transformer design is recommended for OB2263?
faqs.answer: OB2263 works with standard flyback transformer designs. Key considerations include proper core selection for power level, appropriate turns ratio for desired output voltage, and sufficient winding margins for safety isolation. On-Bright provides transformer design guidelines in their application notes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with reference design transformer specifications and adjust based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: How do I set the current limit for OB2263?
faqs.answer: Current limit is set by the current sense resistor connected between the MOSFET source and ground. The CS pin threshold is typically 1V. Calculate Rsense = 1V / Ipeak, where Ipeak is the desired peak current. Include margin for component tolerances.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate sense resistor based on maximum output power and transformer design; verify with actual testing.

faqs.question: What EMI filtering is required with OB2263?
faqs.answer: OB2263's internal frequency jittering reduces conducted EMI, simplifying filter design. A typical common-mode choke with X and Y capacitors is usually sufficient for most applications. Follow CISPR22/EN55022 guidelines for filter component selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with reference design filter values; adjust based on EMI testing results.

products.name: OB2273 QR Flyback Controller
products.shortDescription: Quasi-resonant flyback controller with valley switching technology for high-efficiency power supplies up to 75W with ...
descriptionParagraphs: The OB2273 is a quasi-resonant (QR) flyback controller that achieves high efficiency and low EMI through valley switching operation. It switches the MOSFET at the minimum drain voltage point, reducing switching losses and EMI generation.

The controller features frequency foldback at light loads to maintain high efficiency across the entire load range. Built-in protections include OVP, OCP, OTP, and brown-out protection for reliable operation under all conditions.
OB2273 is well-suited for high-efficiency adapter applications, LED drivers, and auxiliary power supplies where efficiency and EMI performance are critical requirements.

faeReview.content: The OB2273's quasi-resonant operation is a game-changer for efficiency. Valley switching really works - I've measured 2-3% efficiency improvement over traditional PWM controllers at full load, and even more at light loads due to frequency foldback. EMI is noticeably lower, often passing Class B with minimal filtering. The brown-out protection is well-implemented and prevents nuisance trips. One consideration: QR operation requires careful transformer design for proper valley detection. Make sure the auxiliary winding provides clean voltage feedback. For high-efficiency adapters up to 75W, this is my go-to recommendation.
faeReview.author: Principal FAE - High Efficiency Power

alternativeParts.reason: Similar QR flyback controller with skip-cycle mode
alternativeParts.comparison: NCP1337 offers similar QR operation; OB2273 provides better cost efficiency and local support

alternativeParts.reason: QR controller with integrated MOSFET option
alternativeParts.comparison: TEA1733 offers higher integration; OB2273 provides more flexibility in MOSFET selection

companionParts.description: Synchronous rectifier for further efficiency improvement
USB PD controller for modern adapter designs
Standard PWM controller for cost-sensitive designs

faqs.question: What is quasi-resonant operation in OB2273?
faqs.answer: Quasi-resonant operation means the controller switches the MOSFET at the minimum drain voltage point (valley) after the transformer demagnetizes. This reduces switching losses and EMI by switching when voltage and current are at minimum levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use QR operation for high-efficiency applications; standard PWM may be sufficient for cost-sensitive designs.

faqs.question: How does frequency foldback work in OB2273?
faqs.answer: At light loads, OB2273 reduces switching frequency to minimize switching losses while maintaining output regulation. This extends the high-efficiency operating range down to very light loads, important for meeting efficiency standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Frequency foldback helps meet DoE and CoC efficiency requirements; verify audible noise at very light loads.

faqs.question: What is the difference between OB2263 and OB2273?
faqs.answer: OB2263 is a standard PWM controller with fixed frequency operation. OB2273 uses quasi-resonant valley switching for higher efficiency and lower EMI. OB2273 is better for high-efficiency applications; OB2263 is more cost-effective for standard designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose OB2273 for high-efficiency requirements; OB2263 for cost-sensitive standard applications.

faqs.question: How do I design the transformer for QR operation?
faqs.answer: QR transformer design is similar to standard flyback but with attention to leakage inductance and auxiliary winding coupling. Lower leakage inductance provides cleaner valley detection. The transformer should be designed for the desired switching frequency range in QR mode.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow On-Bright transformer design guidelines; pay attention to leakage inductance and winding coupling.

faqs.question: Can OB2273 be used for LED driver applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, OB2273 is well-suited for LED driver applications due to its high efficiency and excellent EMI performance. The constant voltage output can be converted to constant current using external circuitry, or combined with On-Bright's dedicated LED driver ICs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider OB2273 for high-power LED drivers; dedicated LED drivers may be simpler for basic applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between PWM and QR flyback controllers?
faqs.answer: PWM controllers operate at fixed frequency with hard switching, while QR controllers use variable frequency valley switching for reduced switching losses and EMI. QR offers higher efficiency but requires more careful transformer design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose QR for high-efficiency, low-EMI applications; PWM for cost-sensitive, standard designs.

faqs.question: How do I select the right On-Bright AC-DC controller?
faqs.answer: Consider output power, efficiency requirements, EMI specifications, and cost targets. OB2263 series for standard PWM applications; OB2273 series for high-efficiency QR applications. Check power rating and package options for your specific needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match controller to power level and efficiency requirements; consult reference designs for guidance.

faqs.question: What protection features do On-Bright AC-DC controllers include?
faqs.answer: On-Bright controllers include comprehensive protection: OVP (over-voltage), OCP (over-current), OTP (over-temperature), brown-out protection, and short-circuit protection. These ensure safe operation under fault conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify all required protections for your application; additional external protection may be needed for specific requirements.

faqs.question: Can On-Bright controllers meet energy efficiency standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, On-Bright controllers are designed to meet DoE Level VI, CoC Tier 2, and other international efficiency standards. Low standby power and high active mode efficiency help achieve compliance. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference designs and application notes for optimal efficiency; test to verify compliance.

faqs.question: What design resources are available for On-Bright AC-DC products?
faqs.answer: On-Bright provides datasheets, application notes, reference designs, evaluation boards, and transformer design guidelines. Technical support is available through BeiLuo Electronics FAE team. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with reference designs; contact FAE for complex or custom requirements.

faqs.question: What types of power management ICs does On-Bright offer?
faqs.answer: On-Bright offers AC-DC converters for offline power supplies, DC-DC converters for point-of-load regulation, LED drivers for lighting applications, and battery management ICs for charging and protection. Their products cover power ranges from milliwatts to hundreds of watts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select product category based on your application: AC-DC for adapters, DC-DC for POL, LED drivers for lighting, battery management for portable devices.

faqs.question: How does On-Bright compare to other power IC manufacturers?
faqs.answer: On-Bright offers competitive cost-performance ratio with strong local support in China. While international vendors may have broader portfolios, On-Bright excels in specific applications with optimized, cost-effective solutions and faster lead times.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate On-Bright for cost-sensitive designs where their product offerings meet requirements; consider alternatives for specialized applications.

faqs.question: What is the quality and reliability of On-Bright products?
faqs.answer: On-Bright products undergo rigorous testing and quality control. They are qualified for industrial temperature ranges and meet standard reliability requirements. The company follows ISO 9001 quality management systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify appropriate temperature grade for your application; industrial grade suitable for most applications.

faqs.question: Does On-Bright offer automotive-qualified products?
faqs.answer: On-Bright offers products suitable for automotive applications with appropriate qualification. Contact BeiLuo Electronics for specific automotive-grade product availability and AEC-Q100 qualification status.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify automotive requirements when inquiring; verify qualification status for in-vehicle applications.

faqs.question: How can I get samples and evaluation boards?
faqs.answer: Samples and evaluation boards are available through BeiLuo Electronics. Contact our sales team with your requirements. We maintain stock of popular products for quick sample delivery.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request samples early in the design phase for evaluation; order evaluation boards for reference design testing.
